name: Suspicious File Execution via wscript/cscript
description: Detects the execution of scripts (.jse, .vbe, .js, .vba) using wscript.exe or cscript.exe. While legitimate tools, these are often used to execute malicious scripts, bypassing traditional executable defenses. This rule focuses on the execution of these script types, excluding JSON files to reduce false positives.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for Process Creation (1).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that was executed, which should be 'wscript.exe' or 'cscript.exe'.

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the wscript.exe or cscript.exe executable.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used to execute the script, including the file path and extension.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the script was executed.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
